info.title: Demo API (Scalar Galaxy)
info.description:
The Scalar Galaxy is an example OpenAPI document to test OpenAPI tools and libraries. It's a fictional universe with fictional planets and fictional data. Get all the data for [all planets](#tag/planets/get/planets). ## Resources * https://github.com/scalar/scalar * https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification * https://scalar.com ## Markdown Support All descriptions *can* contain ~~tons of text~~ **Markdown**. [If GitHub supports the syntax](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/writing-on-github/getting-started-with-writing-and-formatting-on-github/basic-writing-and-formatting-syntax), chances are we're supporting it, too. You can even create [internal links to reference endpoints](#tag/authentication/post/user/signup).
Examples **Blockquotes** > I love OpenAPI. <3 **Tables** | Feature | Availability | | ---------------- | ------------ | | Markdown Support | ✓ | **Accordion** ```html
Using Details Tags

HTML Example

``` **Images** Yes, there's support for images, too! ![Empty placeholder image showing the width/height](https://images.placeholders.dev/?width=1280&height=720) **Alerts** > [!tip] > You can now use markdown alerts in your descriptions.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Some endpoints are public, but some require authentication. We provide all the required endpoints to create an account and authorize yourself.

tags.name: Celestial Bodies
tags.description: Celestial bodies are the planets and satellites in the Scalar Galaxy.

get.description: It's easy to say you know them all, but do you really? Retrieve all the planets and check whether you missed one.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return

post.description: Time to play god and create a new planet. What do you think? Ah, don't think too much. What could go wrong anyway?

requestBody.description: Information about the newly created planet

200.description: Your server returns this code if it accepts the callback

204.description: Your server should return this HTTP status code if no longer interested in further updates

requestBody.description: Information about the newly exploded planet

get.description: You'll better learn a little bit more about the planets. It might come in handy once space travel is available for everyone.

delete.description: This endpoint was used to delete planets. Unfortunately, that caused a lot of trouble for planets with life. So, this endpoint is now deprecated and should not be used anymore.

200.description: Return a 200 status to indicate that the data was received successfully

planetId.name: planetId
planetId.description: The ID of the planet to get

satelliteId.name: satelliteId
satelliteId.description: The ID of the satellite to get

limit.name: limit
limit.description: The number of items to return

offset.name: offset
offset.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set

Token.description: A token to authenticate a user

CelestialBody.description: A celestial body which can be either a planet or a satellite

Planet.description: A planet in the Scalar Galaxy

examples: A gas giant with a great red spot

x-enum-descriptions.terrestrial: Rocky planets with solid surfaces, like Earth and Mars
x-enum-descriptions.gas_giant: Large planets composed mainly of hydrogen and helium, like Jupiter and Saturn
x-enum-descriptions.ice_giant: Planets composed of water, ammonia, and methane ices, like Uranus and Neptune
x-enum-descriptions.dwarf: Small planetary bodies that don't meet full planet criteria, like Pluto
x-enum-descriptions.super_earth: Rocky planets larger than Earth but smaller than gas giants

habitabilityIndex.description: A score from 0 to 1 indicating potential habitability

additionalProperties.description: Additional physical measurements for the planet

mass.description: Mass in Earth masses (must be greater than 0)

radius.description: Radius in Earth radii (must be greater than 0)

successCallbackUrl.description: URL which gets invoked upon a successful operation

failureCallbackUrl.description: URL which gets invoked upon a failed operation

Satellite.description: Every satellite in the Scalar Galaxy

examples: Phobos is the larger and innermost of the two moons of Mars.

x-enum-descriptions.asteroid: Rocky objects that orbit the sun, typically found in the asteroid belt
x-enum-descriptions.comet: Icy bodies that release gas when approaching the sun, creating visible tails

planetId.description: The ID of the planet this satellite orbits

distance.description: Average distance from the planet in kilometers

imageUrl.description: The URL where the uploaded image can be accessed

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the image was uploaded

fileSize.description: Size of the uploaded image in bytes

mimeType.description: The content type of the uploaded image

examples: You are not authorized to access this resource.
